programme: Emergency Critical Thinking and Mental Readiness

duties.title: Demonstrate situational awareness in dynamic environments
duties.description: Identify, interpret, and project situational cues to maintain an accurate picture of events.

duties.title: Apply structured decision frameworks under time pressure
duties.description: Use OODA and DECIDE models to make sound decisions when information is incomplete and time is short.

duties.title: Execute emergency procedures from trained reflex
duties.description: Perform life-safety procedures accurately even under high physiological arousal.

duties.title: Conduct pre-operational risk analysis
duties.description: Identify failure modes, build mitigations, and establish decision gates before high-stakes events.

duties.title: Debrief and learn from simulated and real incidents
duties.description: Conduct structured After Action Reviews to identify root causes and prevent recurrence.

knowledge.statement: Describe Endsley's three levels of situational awareness and how each is trained.
Explain the OODA loop and its application to non-aviation domains.
Apply the DECIDE model to a novel emergency scenario.
Identify the immediate actions for engine failure below 500 ft AGL.
State the first-line treatment for anaphylaxis and the rationale.
Describe the containment-first principle in cybersecurity incident response.
Conduct a pre-mortem analysis on a given operational plan.
Categorise threats using the TEM (Threat and Error Management) model.
Identify common cognitive biases that degrade emergency decision-making.
Structure an After Action Review using the four-question framework.

skills.statement: Complete an OODA observation phase for a complex scenario, identifying all critical cues.
Score ≥ 80% on a time-pressured DECIDE model exercise.
Choose the correct action in a rapid-decision drill within the allowed time window.
Complete a full emergency simulation scenario with outcome score ≥ 0.7.
List ≥ 5 failure modes for a given plan and propose mitigations for each.
Perform a structured mental rehearsal from a provided script.
Answer Socratic questions at ANALYZE or higher Bloom level on emergency doctrine.
Critically evaluate own performance in an AAR and identify one pattern to change.

behaviours.statement: Remains calm and methodical when scenario pressure escalates.
Seeks competing hypotheses before committing to a course of action.
Communicates clearly and concisely in high-workload situations.
Acknowledges and corrects errors without defensive rationalisation.
Monitors own wellness state and adjusts cognitive load accordingly.
